title: Competing Risks IPCW: Guided Example
code: library(ipcw) library(survival) library(ggplot2) library(purrr)

title: Competing Risks IPCW: Guided Example
text: sim_data_CR() generates competing risks data sub-distribution hazard model. covariate z1 four-level factor (PSA quartile). use baseline-dependent censoring censoring informative.
code: set.seed(9843) dat <- sim_data_CR(   n          = 500,   censoring  = "baseline",   beta1      = log(1.5),   beta2      = log(2.25),   beta3      = log(3.4),   p          = 0.3 ) table(dat$delta) #>  #>  censor event_1 event_2  #>     165     212     123

title: Competing Risks IPCW: Guided Example
code: # Cumulative incidence by PSA quartile plot(survfit(Surv(t, delta) ~ z1, data = dat),      col = 1:4, lty = rep(1:2, each = 4),      xlab = "Time", ylab = "Probability") legend("topleft", legend = paste("z1 =", 0:3), col = 1:4, lty = 1, bty = "n") # Censoring distribution by PSA quartile plot(survfit(Surv(t, delta == "censor") ~ z1, data = dat),      col = 1:4, xlab = "Time", ylab = "P(not censored)") legend("topright", legend = paste("z1 =", 0:3), col = 1:4, lty = 1, bty = "n")

what: Convert to long format and add IPCW weights
title: Competing Risks IPCW: Guided Example
code: dat_long <- wide_to_long_CR(dat)  # Cox model weights dat_long_cox <- add_ipcw_weights(dat_long, strat = "no")  # Stratified (non-parametric) weights dat_long_strat <- add_ipcw_weights(dat_long, strat = "yes") ggplot(dat_long_strat, aes(x = 1 / p_notcens)) +   geom_histogram(bins = 40) +   xlab("IPCW weight") + ylab("Count") +   theme_bw()

title: Competing Risks IPCW: Guided Example
code: esttimes <- sort(dat$t)  ci_naive   <- cuminc_naive(dat, esttimes) ci_wavg    <- cuminc_waverage(dat, esttimes) ci_cox     <- cuminc_ipcw(dat_long_cox, esttimes) to_plot <- rbind(   data.frame(time = esttimes, est = ci_wavg, method = "Weighted average"),   data.frame(time = esttimes, est = ci_cox,  method = "Cox IPCW") )  ggplot(to_plot, aes(x = time, y = est, colour = method)) +   geom_step() +   xlim(0, 5) + ylim(0, 0.65) +   xlab("Time") + ylab("Cumulative incidence of event 1") +   theme_bw(base_size = 14) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank()) #> Warning: Removed 52 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_step()`).

title: Competing Risks IPCW: Guided Example
code: dat_long_fg <- fg_split(dat_long)  # Stratified weights dat_long_fg_strat <- add_fg_weights(dat_long_fg, strat = "yes") fg_strat <- fg_weighted(dat_long_fg_strat, extend = FALSE) exp(fg_strat[, 1])   # exponentiated coefficients #>      z11      z12      z13  #> 1.707717 2.529884 3.168057  # Cox model weights dat_long_fg_cox <- add_fg_weights(dat_long_fg, strat = "no") fg_cox <- fg_weighted(dat_long_fg_cox) exp(fg_cox[, 1]) #>      z11      z12      z13  #> 1.747758 2.574544 3.124305

title: Competing Risks IPCW: Guided Example
text: Bootstrap resampling provides valid standard errors weights estimated. example uses 500 samples; run parallel needed.
code: set.seed(20240917)  boot_dat      <- map(1:500, ~ slice_sample(dat, prop = 1, replace = TRUE)) boot_dat      <- map(boot_dat, function(x) { x$id <- seq_len(nrow(x)); x }) boot_dat_long <- map(boot_dat, wide_to_long_CR)  # Weighted-average cumulative incidence bootstrap w_avg_boot <- map(boot_dat, ~ cuminc_waverage(., esttimes)) w_avg_boot <- matrix(unlist(w_avg_boot), ncol = length(esttimes), byrow = TRUE) lower_wavg  <- apply(w_avg_boot, 2, function(x)                  ifelse(any(is.na(x)), NA, quantile(x, 0.025))) upper_wavg  <- apply(w_avg_boot, 2, function(x)                  ifelse(any(is.na(x)), NA, quantile(x, 0.975)))  # Cox IPCW cumulative incidence bootstrap cox_boot      <- map(boot_dat_long, ~ cuminc_ipcw(add_ipcw_weights(., strat = "no"), esttimes)) cox_boot      <- matrix(unlist(cox_boot), ncol = length(esttimes), byrow = TRUE) lower_cox     <- apply(cox_boot, 2, function(x)                    ifelse(any(is.na(x)), NA, quantile(x, 0.025))) upper_cox     <- apply(cox_boot, 2, function(x)                    ifelse(any(is.na(x)), NA, quantile(x, 0.975)))  # Fine-Gray stratified bootstrap boot_dat_fg   <- map(boot_dat_long, fg_split) fg_strat_boot <- map(boot_dat_fg, ~ fg_weighted(add_fg_weights(., strat = "yes"),                                                  extend = FALSE)[, 1]) fg_strat_boot <- matrix(unlist(fg_strat_boot), ncol = 3, byrow = TRUE) lower_fg_strat <- apply(fg_strat_boot, 2, function(x)                      ifelse(any(is.na(x)), NA, quantile(x, 0.025))) upper_fg_strat <- apply(fg_strat_boot, 2, function(x)                      ifelse(any(is.na(x)), NA, quantile(x, 0.975)))  exp(fg_strat[, 1]) exp(lower_fg_strat) exp(upper_fg_strat)

title: Single-Event IPCW: Guided Example
code: library(ipcw) library(survival) library(purrr)

title: Single-Event IPCW: Guided Example
text: package ships single_example_dat, simulated dataset 500 subjects generated set.seed(20240429). key variables : t: observed time (event censoring) delta: event indicator (1 = event, 0 = censored) x: treatment (0 = chemotherapy, 1 = TKI) W2: ancillary biomarker drives informative censoring code re-creates dataset scratch, can see exactly generated.
code: data(single_example_dat) head(single_example_dat) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>       S      t delta     x     W2 #>   <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <int>  <dbl> #> 1  427.  427.      1     0 0.533  #> 2 1541. 1541.      1     1 0.986  #> 3  417.  417.      1     0 0.579  #> 4 1095.  327.      0     1 0.701  #> 5  108.   51.5     0     0 0.0469 #> 6  768.  768.      1     0 0.889 # Fix simulation parameters n       <- 500 alpha   <- 0.05 x_prop  <- 0.5 a       <- 2 sigma   <- 500 beta    <- log(0.25) lambda  <- 0.01 phi     <- -5  set.seed(20240429)  Y1 <- rexp(n, rate = 1) Y2 <- rexp(n, rate = 1) Z  <- rgamma(n, shape = alpha, rate = 1) W1 <- (1 + Y1 / Z)^(-alpha) W2 <- (1 + Y2 / Z)^(-alpha) x  <- rbinom(n, 1, prob = x_prop)  S     <- sigma * (-log(1 - W1) / exp(beta * x))^(1 / a) C     <- rexp(n, rate = lambda * exp(phi * W2)) t     <- pmin(S, C) delta <- as.integer(S <= C)  single_example_dat <- tibble::tibble(S, t, delta, x, W2)

what: Prepare data and compute IPCW weights
title: Single-Event IPCW: Guided Example
text: get_ipcw_wgt() converts wide dataset counting-process (long) format appends unstabilized IPCW weight column wgt. package also provides single_example_ipcw_dat weights pre-computed. Alternatively, compute weights directly:
code: data(single_example_ipcw_dat) head(single_example_ipcw_dat) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   id_nest true_time     x    W2    id tstart tstop delta censor inv_wgt   wgt #>     <int>     <dbl> <int> <dbl> <int>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1       1      427.     0 0.533     1  0     0.688     0      0   1      1    #> 2       1      427.     0 0.533     1  0.688 1.28      0      0   0.999  1.00 #> 3       1      427.     0 0.533     1  1.28  2.77      0      0   0.999  1.00 #> 4       1      427.     0 0.533     1  2.77  5.15      0      0   0.998  1.00 #> 5       1      427.     0 0.533     1  5.15  5.79      0      0   0.997  1.00 #> 6       1      427.     0 0.533     1  5.79  6.18      0      0   0.997  1.00 dat_long <- get_ipcw_wgt(single_example_dat)

title: Single-Event IPCW: Guided Example
text: chunk requires ggsurvfit package. installed, can plot IPCW K-M curves base R instead (see commented code).
code: library(ggsurvfit) #> Loading required package: ggplot2 km_fit2 <- survfit2(Surv(tstart, tstop, delta) ~ x,                     data    = single_example_ipcw_dat,                     weights = single_example_ipcw_dat$wgt,                     timefix = FALSE) ggsurvfit(km_fit2) +   xlim(c(0, 1000)) +   scale_color_hue(labels = c("Chemotherapy", "TKI")) +   labs(     x = "Days from treatment start",     y = "Progression-free survival probability"   ) #> Warning: Removed 81 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_step()`). km_fit <- survfit(Surv(tstart, tstop, delta) ~ x,                   data    = single_example_ipcw_dat,                   weights = wgt,                   timefix = FALSE) plot(km_fit, col = 1:2, xlim = c(0, 1000),      xlab = "Days from treatment start",      ylab = "Progression-free survival probability") legend("topright", legend = c("Chemotherapy", "TKI"), col = 1:2, lty = 1)

title: Single-Event IPCW: Guided Example
text: use convenience wrapper, also returns hazard ratio robust-SE-based confidence interval:
code: ipcw_cox_fit <- coxph(   Surv(tstart, tstop, delta) ~ x + cluster(id),   data    = single_example_ipcw_dat,   weights = single_example_ipcw_dat$wgt,   timefix = FALSE ) summary(ipcw_cox_fit)$coef #>        coef exp(coef)  se(coef) robust se         z    Pr(>|z|) #> x -1.209459 0.2983585 0.1029929  0.162079 -7.462158 8.51167e-14 get_ipcw_cox_fit(single_example_ipcw_dat, weight = "wgt") #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   term  log_hr log_hr_se log_hr_rob_se    hr hr_ci_low hr_ci_high #>   <chr>  <dbl>     <dbl>         <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 x      -1.21     0.103         0.162 0.298     0.217      0.410

title: Single-Event IPCW: Guided Example
text: Bootstrap resampling needed valid confidence intervals weights estimated. code runs 500 bootstrap samples; practice may want run parallel.
code: set.seed(20240917) B <- 500  ipcw_boot_dat <- map(1:B, ~ dplyr::slice_sample(single_example_dat,                                                   prop = 1, replace = TRUE)) ipcw_boot_dat <- map(ipcw_boot_dat, get_ipcw_wgt)  ipcw_cox_boot <- map(ipcw_boot_dat, ~ get_ipcw_cox_fit(., "wgt")) boot_log_hrs  <- map_dbl(ipcw_cox_boot, "log_hr")  boot_pci <- get_boot_pci(data.frame(log_hr = boot_log_hrs))  result_tab <- data.frame(   Scale          = c("log(HR)", "HR"),   Estimate       = c(coef(ipcw_cox_fit), exp(coef(ipcw_cox_fit))),   Lower_95pct_PI = c(boot_pci[[1]], exp(boot_pci[[1]])),   Upper_95pct_PI = c(boot_pci[[2]], exp(boot_pci[[2]])) ) print(round(result_tab[, -1], 2))

title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {ipcw: Inverse Probability of Censoring Weighted Methods for Survival Analysis},   author = {Emily Zabor and Anne Eaton},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://zabore.github.io/ipcw}, }

what: Add Fine-Gray IPCW weights to Fine-Gray split data — add_fg_weights
title: Add Fine-Gray IPCW weights to Fine-Gray split data — add_fg_weights
text: Computes probability remaining uncensored type-2 event appends column p_notcens_after_death.

title: Add Fine-Gray IPCW weights to Fine-Gray split data — add_fg_weights
code: add_fg_weights(data_long_fg, strat = "no")

title: Add Fine-Gray IPCW weights to Fine-Gray split data — add_fg_weights
text: data_long_fg data frame Fine-Gray format, returned fg_split(). strat Character. Passed add_ipcw_weights(). "" (default) uses Cox model; "yes" uses stratum-specific KM estimates.

title: Add Fine-Gray IPCW weights to Fine-Gray split data — add_fg_weights
text: data_long_fg additional column p_notcens_after_death.

title: Add Fine-Gray IPCW weights to Fine-Gray split data — add_fg_weights
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_CR(n = 100, censoring = "baseline") dat_long    <- wide_to_long_CR(dat) dat_long_fg <- fg_split(dat_long) dat_long_fg <- add_fg_weights(dat_long_fg, strat = "no") summary(dat_long_fg$p_notcens_after_death) #>      Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max.  #> 0.0002632 1.0000000 1.0000000 0.9525524 1.0000000 1.0000000

what: Add IPCW weights to competing risks long-format data — add_ipcw_weights
title: Add IPCW weights to competing risks long-format data — add_ipcw_weights
text: Estimates probability remaining uncensored appends column p_notcens dataset. Supports two estimation strategies: Cox proportional hazards model (strat = "") non-parametric (KM) estimates within level z1 (strat = "yes").

title: Add IPCW weights to competing risks long-format data — add_ipcw_weights
code: add_ipcw_weights(data_long, strat = "no", new_data = NULL, by.start = TRUE)

title: Add IPCW weights to competing risks long-format data — add_ipcw_weights
text: data_long data frame long format, returned wide_to_long_CR(). Must contain columns z1, tstart, tstop, censor. strat Character. "" (default) fits single Cox model censoring distribution using z1 covariate. "yes" estimates censoring distribution non-parametrically within stratum z1. new_data Optional data frame weights applied. NULL (default), weights computed data_long . .start Logical. TRUE (default), weight P(censored tstart). FALSE, weight P(censored interval tstart tstop).

title: Add IPCW weights to competing risks long-format data — add_ipcw_weights
text: new_data (data_long new_data NULL) additional column p_notcens.

title: Add IPCW weights to competing risks long-format data — add_ipcw_weights
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_CR(n = 100, censoring = "baseline") dat_long <- wide_to_long_CR(dat) dat_long <- add_ipcw_weights(dat_long, strat = "no") summary(dat_long$p_notcens) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>  0.0547  0.7641  0.9126  0.8413  0.9757  1.0000

what: Cox model IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_ipcw
title: Cox model IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_ipcw
text: Estimates marginal cumulative incidence event type 1 using weighted Aalen-Johansen estimator, weights IPCW weights stored p_notcens column (added add_ipcw_weights()).

title: Cox model IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_ipcw
code: cuminc_ipcw(   data_long,   esttimes = seq(from = 0, to = 10, length.out = 100),   extend = TRUE )

title: Cox model IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_ipcw
text: data_long long-format data frame IPCW weights, returned add_ipcw_weights(). Must contain columns tstart, tstop, delta, id, p_notcens. esttimes Numeric vector times return estimates. Defaults 100 equally spaced points 0 10. extend Logical. FALSE, estimates beyond minimum stratum-specific maximum follow-times set NA. Default TRUE.

title: Cox model IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_ipcw
text: numeric vector IPCW cumulative incidence estimates esttimes.

title: Cox model IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_ipcw
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_CR(n = 200, censoring = "baseline") dat_long <- wide_to_long_CR(dat) dat_long <- add_ipcw_weights(dat_long, strat = "no") #> Warning: Loglik converged before variable  3 ; beta may be infinite.  cuminc_ipcw(dat_long, esttimes = seq(0, 5, 0.5)) #>  [1] 0.0000000 0.2525084 0.3656652 0.4349979 0.4489364 0.4768644 0.4768644 #>  [8] 0.4768644 0.4847719 0.4847719 0.4847719

title: Naive (unweighted) cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_naive
text: Estimates cumulative incidence event type 1 using standard Aalen-Johansen estimator without IPCW adjustment. Serves comparison method censoring independent covariates.

title: Naive (unweighted) cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_naive
code: cuminc_naive(dat, esttimes)

title: Naive (unweighted) cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_naive
text: dat wide-format competing risks data frame columns t delta (factor levels "censor", "event_1", "event_2"). esttimes Numeric vector times return estimates.

title: Naive (unweighted) cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_naive
text: numeric vector cumulative incidence estimates esttimes.

title: Naive (unweighted) cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_naive

what: Weighted-average (non-parametric) IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_waverage
title: Weighted-average (non-parametric) IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_waverage
text: Estimates marginal cumulative incidence event type 1 computing stratum-specific Aalen-Johansen estimates (stratified z1) combining sample-proportion weights. non-parametric IPCW approach.

title: Weighted-average (non-parametric) IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_waverage
code: cuminc_waverage(dat, esttimes = seq(from = 0, to = 10, length.out = 100))

title: Weighted-average (non-parametric) IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_waverage
text: dat wide-format competing risks data frame columns t, delta, z1. esttimes Numeric vector times return estimates. Defaults 100 equally spaced points 0 10.

title: Weighted-average (non-parametric) IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_waverage
text: numeric vector weighted-average cumulative incidence estimates esttimes. Values beyond minimum stratum-specific maximum follow-times set NA.

title: Weighted-average (non-parametric) IPCW cumulative incidence estimate — cuminc_waverage

what: Naive Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_naive
title: Naive Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_naive
text: Fits Fine-Gray model using standard survival::finegray() approach, without IPCW adjustment. Serves comparison fg_weighted().

title: Naive Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_naive
code: fg_naive(dat)

title: Naive Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_naive
text: dat wide-format competing risks data frame columns t, delta, z1.

title: Naive Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_naive
text: matrix one row per term two columns: log sub-distribution hazard ratio standard error.

title: Naive Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_naive
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_CR(n = 200, censoring = "independent") fg_naive(dat) #>          coef  se(coef) #> z11 0.9452312 0.3624174 #> z12 1.3652479 0.3799441 #> z13 1.7795374 0.3446775

what: Prepare long-format data for Fine-Gray weighted regression — fg_split
title: Prepare long-format data for Fine-Gray weighted regression — fg_split
text: type-2 event (event_2), subjects artificially re-entered risk set (Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard model). function appends additional rows, split every censoring time.

title: Prepare long-format data for Fine-Gray weighted regression — fg_split
code: fg_split(data_long)

title: Prepare long-format data for Fine-Gray weighted regression — fg_split
text: data_long long-format data frame, returned wide_to_long_CR().

title: Prepare long-format data for Fine-Gray weighted regression — fg_split
text: data frame Fine-Gray format, additional rows subjects experienced event_2, sorted id tstart.

title: Prepare long-format data for Fine-Gray weighted regression — fg_split
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_CR(n = 100, censoring = "baseline") dat_long <- wide_to_long_CR(dat) dat_long_fg <- fg_split(dat_long) nrow(dat_long_fg) > nrow(dat_long) #> [1] TRUE

what: IPCW-weighted Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_weighted
title: IPCW-weighted Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_weighted
text: Fits Fine-Gray model Fine-Gray split dataset, weighting inverse probability remaining uncensored event_2 (p_notcens_after_death), uses robust sandwich variance via cluster(id).

title: IPCW-weighted Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_weighted
code: fg_weighted(data_long_fg, extend = TRUE)

title: IPCW-weighted Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_weighted
text: data_long_fg data frame Fine-Gray format weights, returned add_fg_weights(). extend Logical. FALSE, data truncated minimum stratum-specific maximum follow-times fitting. Default TRUE.

title: IPCW-weighted Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_weighted
text: matrix one row per term two columns: log sub-distribution hazard ratio robust standard error.

title: IPCW-weighted Fine-Gray sub-distribution hazard regression — fg_weighted
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_CR(n = 200, censoring = "baseline") dat_long    <- wide_to_long_CR(dat) dat_long_fg <- fg_split(dat_long) dat_long_fg <- add_fg_weights(dat_long_fg, strat = "no") #> Warning: Loglik converged before variable  3 ; beta may be infinite.  fg_weighted(dat_long_fg) #>         coef robust se #> z11 1.364480 0.5617613 #> z12 1.798696 0.5626535 #> z13 2.222293 0.5323173

what: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval for the log hazard ratio — get_boot_pci
title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval for the log hazard ratio — get_boot_pci
text: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval log hazard ratio

title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval for the log hazard ratio — get_boot_pci
code: get_boot_pci(data)

title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval for the log hazard ratio — get_boot_pci
text: data data frame column log_hr containing bootstrap log hazard ratio estimates, returned multiple calls get_ipcw_cox_fit() get_cox_fit().

title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval for the log hazard ratio — get_boot_pci
text: named numeric vector length 2 giving 2.5th 97.5th percentiles bootstrap distribution.

title: Compute bootstrap percentile confidence interval for the log hazard ratio — get_boot_pci
code: boot_results <- data.frame(log_hr = rnorm(500, mean = -0.5, sd = 0.2)) get_boot_pci(boot_results) #>       2.5%      97.5%  #> -0.9221499 -0.1397219

what: Compute bootstrap variance of the log hazard ratio — get_boot_var
title: Compute bootstrap variance of the log hazard ratio — get_boot_var
text: Compute bootstrap variance log hazard ratio

title: Compute bootstrap variance of the log hazard ratio — get_boot_var
code: get_boot_var(data, B)

title: Compute bootstrap variance of the log hazard ratio — get_boot_var
text: data data frame column log_hr containing bootstrap log hazard ratio estimates, returned multiple calls get_ipcw_cox_fit() get_cox_fit(). B Integer. Number bootstrap samples used.

title: Compute bootstrap variance of the log hazard ratio — get_boot_var
text: single numeric value: bootstrap variance estimate.

title: Compute bootstrap variance of the log hazard ratio — get_boot_var
code: # Toy example with 10 simulated bootstrap log HRs boot_results <- data.frame(log_hr = rnorm(10, mean = -0.5, sd = 0.2)) get_boot_var(boot_results, B = 10) #> [1] 0.04568865

what: Fit a standard (unweighted) Cox proportional hazards model — get_cox_fit
title: Fit a standard (unweighted) Cox proportional hazards model — get_cox_fit
text: Convenience wrapper around survival::coxph() covariate x using counting-process (tstart, tstop) time variables. Returns log-hazard ratio estimates standard errors exponentiated hazard ratio 95% confidence intervals.

title: Fit a standard (unweighted) Cox proportional hazards model — get_cox_fit
code: get_cox_fit(data)

title: Fit a standard (unweighted) Cox proportional hazards model — get_cox_fit
text: data data frame long (counting-process) format containing columns tstart, tstop, delta, x.

title: Fit a standard (unweighted) Cox proportional hazards model — get_cox_fit
text: data frame one row per term containing: term Covariate name. log_hr Log hazard ratio estimate. log_hr_se Standard error log hazard ratio. hr Hazard ratio estimate. hr_ci_low Lower 95% confidence limit. hr_ci_high Upper 95% confidence limit.

title: Fit a standard (unweighted) Cox proportional hazards model — get_cox_fit
code: data(single_example_ipcw_dat) get_cox_fit(single_example_ipcw_dat) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   term  log_hr log_hr_se    hr hr_ci_low hr_ci_high #>   <chr>  <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 x      -1.85     0.147 0.157     0.117      0.209

what: Fit an IPCW-weighted Cox proportional hazards model — get_ipcw_cox_fit
title: Fit an IPCW-weighted Cox proportional hazards model — get_ipcw_cox_fit
text: Fits weighted Cox model robust sandwich variance returns log-hazard ratio estimates together model-based robust standard errors, plus exponentiated hazard ratio 95% confidence interval based robust standard error.

title: Fit an IPCW-weighted Cox proportional hazards model — get_ipcw_cox_fit
code: get_ipcw_cox_fit(data, weight)

title: Fit an IPCW-weighted Cox proportional hazards model — get_ipcw_cox_fit
text: data data frame long (counting-process) format, returned get_ipcw_wgt(). Must contain columns tstart, tstop, delta, id, weight column named weight. weight character string giving name weight column data.

title: Fit an IPCW-weighted Cox proportional hazards model — get_ipcw_cox_fit
text: data frame one row per term containing: term Covariate name. log_hr Log hazard ratio estimate. log_hr_se Model-based standard error log hazard ratio. log_hr_rob_se Robust (sandwich) standard error log hazard ratio. hr Hazard ratio estimate. hr_ci_low Lower 95% confidence limit (based robust SE). hr_ci_high Upper 95% confidence limit (based robust SE).

title: Fit an IPCW-weighted Cox proportional hazards model — get_ipcw_cox_fit
code: data(single_example_ipcw_dat) get_ipcw_cox_fit(single_example_ipcw_dat, weight = "wgt") #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   term  log_hr log_hr_se log_hr_rob_se    hr hr_ci_low hr_ci_high #>   <chr>  <dbl>     <dbl>         <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 x      -1.21     0.103         0.162 0.298     0.217      0.410

what: Estimate IPCW Kaplan-Meier survival probabilities by binary covariate — get_ipcw_km_prob_x
title: Estimate IPCW Kaplan-Meier survival probabilities by binary covariate — get_ipcw_km_prob_x
text: Fits weighted Kaplan-Meier estimator stratified binary covariate x returns survival probabilities evaluated pre-specified set times.

title: Estimate IPCW Kaplan-Meier survival probabilities by binary covariate — get_ipcw_km_prob_x
code: get_ipcw_km_prob_x(data, pre_times = seq(0, 50, 1))

title: Estimate IPCW Kaplan-Meier survival probabilities by binary covariate — get_ipcw_km_prob_x
text: data data frame long (counting-process) format IPCW weight column wgt, returned get_ipcw_wgt(). pre_times Numeric vector times evaluate survival probabilities. Defaults seq(0, 50, 1).

title: Estimate IPCW Kaplan-Meier survival probabilities by binary covariate — get_ipcw_km_prob_x
text: tibble columns time, surv (survival probability), x (stratum).

title: Estimate IPCW Kaplan-Meier survival probabilities by binary covariate — get_ipcw_km_prob_x
code: data(single_example_ipcw_dat) get_ipcw_km_prob_x(single_example_ipcw_dat, pre_times = seq(0, 500, 10)) #> # A tibble: 102 × 3 #>     time  surv x     #>    <dbl> <dbl> <fct> #>  1     0 1     x=0   #>  2    10 1     x=0   #>  3    20 0.995 x=0   #>  4    30 0.995 x=0   #>  5    40 0.989 x=0   #>  6    50 0.989 x=0   #>  7    60 0.973 x=0   #>  8    70 0.965 x=0   #>  9    80 0.956 x=0   #> 10    90 0.947 x=0   #> # ℹ 92 more rows

what: Compute IPCW weights for single-event survival data — get_ipcw_wgt
title: Compute IPCW weights for single-event survival data — get_ipcw_wgt
text: Fits Cox model censoring distribution returns original dataset counting-process (long) format unstabilized IPCW weight column appended. dataset must contain columns named t (event/censoring time), delta (event indicator, 1 = event), W2 (covariate used model censoring distribution).

title: Compute IPCW weights for single-event survival data — get_ipcw_wgt
code: get_ipcw_wgt(data)

title: Compute IPCW weights for single-event survival data — get_ipcw_wgt
text: data data frame columns t, delta, W2, produced data-generation code package vignette.

title: Compute IPCW weights for single-event survival data — get_ipcw_wgt
text: data frame long (counting-process) format columns tstart, tstop, delta, id, wgt (unstabilized IPCW weight), original columns.

title: Compute IPCW weights for single-event survival data — get_ipcw_wgt

what: Simulate competing risks survival data — sim_data_CR
title: Simulate competing risks survival data — sim_data_CR
text: Generates dataset two competing events sub-distribution hazard model parameterized four-level baseline covariate z1. Optionally adds independent covariate-dependent administrative censoring.

title: Simulate competing risks survival data — sim_data_CR
code: sim_data_CR(   n = 100,   censoring = "none",   beta1 = log(1.5),   beta2 = log(2.25),   beta3 = log(3.4),   p = 0.3 )

title: Simulate competing risks survival data — sim_data_CR
text: n Integer. Number subjects simulate. Default 100. censoring Character string specifying censoring mechanism. One "none", "independent", "baseline" (censoring rate depends z1). Default "none". beta1 Numeric. Log sub-distribution hazard ratio z1 = 1 vs z1 = 0 event type 1. Default log(1.5). beta2 Numeric. Log sub-distribution hazard ratio z1 = 2 vs z1 = 0 event type 1. Default log(2.25). beta3 Numeric. Log sub-distribution hazard ratio z1 = 3 vs z1 = 0 event type 1. Default log(3.4). p Numeric (0, 1). Baseline probability event type 1 reference group (z1 = 0). Default 0.3.

title: Simulate competing risks survival data — sim_data_CR
text: data frame columns: z1 Four-level factor covariate (levels 0-3). delta Factor event indicator levels "censor", "event_1", "event_2". t Event censoring time.

title: Simulate competing risks survival data — sim_data_CR
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_CR(n = 200, censoring = "baseline") table(dat$delta) #>  #>  censor event_1 event_2  #>      74      85      41

what: Simulate single-event survival data with informative censoring — sim_data_SE
title: Simulate single-event survival data with informative censoring — sim_data_SE
text: Generates dataset event times follow Weibull distribution parameterized binary treatment covariate x, censoring depends ancillary biomarker W2 shared gamma frailty, inducing informative censoring. data-generating mechanism used single-event guided example.

title: Simulate single-event survival data with informative censoring — sim_data_SE
code: sim_data_SE(   n = 500,   alpha = 0.05,   x_prop = 0.5,   a = 2,   sigma = 500,   beta = log(0.25),   lambda = 0.01,   phi = -5 )

title: Simulate single-event survival data with informative censoring — sim_data_SE
text: n Integer. Number subjects simulate. Default 500. alpha Numeric. Shape parameter gamma frailty distribution, also used Pareto exponent W1 W2. Default 0.05. x_prop Numeric (0, 1). Probability treatment (x = 1). Default 0.5. Numeric. Weibull shape parameter event time distribution. Default 2. sigma Numeric. Weibull scale parameter event time distribution. Default 500. beta Numeric. Log hazard ratio treatment effect event time. Default log(0.25). lambda Numeric. Baseline censoring rate. Default 0.01. phi Numeric. Effect W2 censoring rate (log scale). Negative values mean higher W2 leads lower censoring rate (.e., longer follow-high-W2 subjects). Default -5.

title: Simulate single-event survival data with informative censoring — sim_data_SE
text: data frame columns: S True (latent) event time. t Observed time (minimum S censoring time). delta Event indicator: 1 = event occurred, 0 = censored. x Binary treatment indicator (0 1). W2 Ancillary biomarker covariate drives informative censoring.

title: Simulate single-event survival data with informative censoring — sim_data_SE
code: set.seed(20240429) dat <- sim_data_SE(n = 500) table(dat$delta) #>  #>   0   1  #> 198 302  summary(dat$t) #>      Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max.  #>    0.6884  172.7074  441.1759  552.9262  803.2563 2429.5429

title: Single-event example dataset — single_example_dat
text: simulated dataset used single-event IPCW guided example. represents clinical scenario event interest progression-free survival, binary treatment covariate x distinguishes two treatment arms, W2 informative censoring covariate (analogous ancillary biomarker). Censoring depends W2, making standard Kaplan-Meier Cox analyses potentially biased.

title: Single-event example dataset — single_example_dat
code: data(single_example_dat)

title: Single-event example dataset — single_example_dat
text: tibble 500 rows 5 columns: S True (latent) event time. t Observed time (minimum S censoring time). delta Event indicator: 1 = event occurred, 0 = censored. x Binary treatment indicator: 0 = chemotherapy, 1 = TKI. W2 Continuous covariate drives informative censoring (analogous ancillary biomarker).

title: Single-event example dataset — single_example_dat
text: Generated authors via sim_data_SE() set.seed(20240429) default parameters.

title: Single-event example dataset — single_example_dat
text: dataset generated following parameters: \(n = 500\), \(\alpha = 0.05\), \(\sigma = 500\), \(\beta = \log(0.25)\), \(\lambda = 0.01\), \(\phi = -5\). set.seed(20240429). See single-event vignette full data-generation code.

what: Single-event example dataset in IPCW long format — single_example_ipcw_dat
title: Single-event example dataset in IPCW long format — single_example_ipcw_dat
text: single_example_dat dataset converted counting-process (long) format IPCW weights appended, produced data-preparation steps single-event vignette (equivalently get_ipcw_wgt()).

title: Single-event example dataset in IPCW long format — single_example_ipcw_dat
code: data(single_example_ipcw_dat)

title: Single-event example dataset in IPCW long format — single_example_ipcw_dat
text: data frame long format one rows per subject columns including: id_nest Nested simulation identifier. id Subject identifier. true_time True (latent) event time used simulation diagnostics. tstart Left endpoint time interval. tstop Right endpoint time interval. delta Event indicator current interval. censor Censoring indicator current interval. x Binary treatment indicator. W2 Ancillary biomarker covariate. inv_wgt Estimated probability remaining uncensored (inverse weight). wgt Unstabilized IPCW weight (reciprocal inv_wgt).

title: Single-event example dataset in IPCW long format — single_example_ipcw_dat
text: Generated authors via get_ipcw_wgt() applied single_example_dat.

what: Convert wide competing risks data to long (counting-process) format — wide_to_long_CR
title: Convert wide competing risks data to long (counting-process) format — wide_to_long_CR
text: Splits wide dataset every censoring time, creating one rows per subject. resulting long dataset suitable fitting Cox models censoring distribution use add_ipcw_weights().

title: Convert wide competing risks data to long (counting-process) format — wide_to_long_CR
code: wide_to_long_CR(dat)

title: Convert wide competing risks data to long (counting-process) format — wide_to_long_CR
text: dat data frame columns t (event/censoring time), delta (factor levels "censor", "event_1", "event_2"), z1 (covariate). Typically output sim_data_CR().

title: Convert wide competing risks data to long (counting-process) format — wide_to_long_CR
text: data frame long format columns id, z1, delta, censor, event2_time, tstart, tstop.

title: Convert wide competing risks data to long (counting-process) format — wide_to_long_CR
code: set.seed(42) dat <- sim_data_CR(n = 100, censoring = "baseline") dat_long <- wide_to_long_CR(dat) head(dat_long) #>   z1 id censor event2_time       tstart        tstop  delta #> 1  0  1      0          NA 0.0000000000 0.0008870787 censor #> 2  0  1      0          NA 0.0008870787 0.0236331037 censor #> 3  0  1      0          NA 0.0236331037 0.0295500713 censor #> 4  0  1      0          NA 0.0295500713 0.0724101320 censor #> 5  0  1      0          NA 0.0724101320 0.0812450008 censor #> 6  0  1      0          NA 0.0812450008 0.1824410379 censor
